research.valueProp: Loop Data turns warehouse models into governed activation surfaces. Joint with ABC, customers move from a Snowflake or BigQuery model to a live workflow inside ABC without brittle reverse-ETL — cutting time-to-activation from weeks to hours.
research.idealCustomerProfile: Mid-market data and revenue teams (300–3,000 employees) running Snowflake/BigQuery with a downstream activation gap. Strong fit when there's an analytics engineer in seat and at least one revenue system already integrated with ABC.
research.archetypeRationale: Loop Data's value lands inside ABC through a technical integration. Sales motion is integration-first, monetization typically via API fee, primary KPI is product adoption and retention.
research.scope: Phase 1 (60 days): production-grade Loop ↔ ABC integration, joint landing page, one anchor customer reference. Phase 2: in-product cross-promotion + quarterly business reviews.
research.competitiveLandscape: Sits alongside generic reverse-ETL tools but wins on governance and lineage on the activation path. Few competitors integrate this tightly into ABC.
riskFlags: Security review needed for warehouse credentials

internalAwareness.subject: [Partnerships] We've signed Loop Data — Data Affiliate
internalAwareness.body:
## Overview
We've signed **Loop Data** ([loopdata.io](https://loopdata.io)) as a new Data Affiliate partner. The integration moves into onboarding this week with primary contact **Priya Shah** (priya@loopdata.io).

## Value Prop
Loop Data turns warehouse models into governed activation surfaces. Joint with ABC, customers move from a Snowflake/BigQuery model to a live workflow inside ABC without brittle reverse-ETL.

## Ideal Customer Profile
Mid-market data and revenue teams (300–3,000 employees) on Snowflake/BigQuery with a downstream activation gap.

## Partner Archetype — Data Affiliate
Loop Data's value lands inside ABC through a technical integration. Integration-first sales motion. Monetization via API fee.

## Scope
Phase 1 (60 days): production-grade integration, joint landing page, one anchor reference. Phase 2: in-product cross-promotion + QBRs.

## What to expect next
- Partner-facing launch chat goes out today.
- The agent will follow up if the partner is unresponsive at 10/20/30 days.
- Once intake closes, we'll publish a launch timeline with three communications.

Reply here with edits or call out blockers before we hit "Approve & Launch to Partner".

research.valueProp: Northbeam is a fast-growing GTM advisory shop that lands ABC inside mid-market deals via outbound referral. Their team brings warm intros and senior-level relationships across the revenue stack.
research.idealCustomerProfile: Mid-market companies (500–5,000 employees) actively re-platforming their revenue stack, where Northbeam is already advising on tooling decisions.
research.archetypeRationale: Northbeam's strongest motion is outbound referral. Value: higher close rates and lower churn. Revenue share monetization.
research.scope: Phase 1: 5 anchor referrals in 60 days + co-branded one-pager. Phase 2: shared QBR cadence + jointly run executive briefings.
research.competitiveLandscape: Competing advisory shops exist, but Northbeam's tight integration into ABC's deal cycle differentiates.
riskFlags: Referral attribution flow not yet wired

Outbound referral, revenue share monetization, primary KPI: win rate and ARR.

## What to expect next
Partner-facing chat going out today; we'll set a target launch date and confirm the attribution flow.

messages.content:
Hi Marcus 👋

I'm ABC's Partner Launch Agent. Congrats on signing with ABC.

We'll capture your **Partner Launch Intake** here so we can fill the launch templates (Partner Data Brief, Integration Guide, decks, and FAQs) for your team.

Take your time — you can attach files where helpful (logos, one-pagers, etc.).

**Partner basics (all templates)** · Question 1 of 42

What is the partner name?
Northbeam Advisors

**Partner basics (all templates)** · Question 2 of 42

In one sentence, what does the partner do?
Fast-growing GTM advisory shop that lands ABC inside mid-market deals via outbound referral.

**Partner basics (all templates)** · Question 3 of 42

What type of partnership is this?

Options: Data Affiliate · Referral · Platform · Channel
Referral

**Partner basics (all templates)** · Question 4 of 42

What is the planned partner launch date?

_Use an ISO date (2026-06-01) or a phrase like “early June”._
early June

**Partner basics (all templates)** · Question 5 of 42

Upload the partner logo or provide a logo URL.

_Paste a URL, describe the file, or use the paperclip to attach._
describe the file

**Scope (Data Brief, decks, FAQ)** · Question 6 of 42

Which ABC products does this partner support?

Options (reply with one line, comma-separated): Ignite, GloFox, Trainerize, Evo, GymSales
Ignite

**Scope (Data Brief, decks, FAQ)** · Question 7 of 42

Which customer segments are targeted?

Options (reply with one line, comma-separated): Gym, Studio, Coach
Gym

**Scope (Data Brief, decks, FAQ)** · Question 8 of 42

Which sub-segments apply?

Options (reply with one line, comma-separated): SMB (1 location), MML (2–40 locations), GES (40+ locations)
MML

**Scope (Data Brief, decks, FAQ)** · Question 9 of 42

Which geographies are supported?

Options (reply with one line, comma-separated): US, Canada, LatAm, EMEA, APAC, Other
US

**Scope (Data Brief, decks, FAQ)** · Question 10 of 42

If Other, specify the supported geography.
na

**Value & positioning (customer deck, FAQ, ICP)** · Question 11 of 42

What business problems does this partner solve for operators or gym owners?

_Include outcomes like reduced manual work, new revenue, improved retention, better member experience, etc._
reduced manual work, new revenue, improved retention, better member experience, etc.

**Value & positioning (customer deck, FAQ, ICP)** · Question 12 of 42

How does this improve the member experience?
nothing

**Value & positioning (customer deck, FAQ, ICP)** · Question 13 of 42

What customer pain points or complaints indicate a strong fit?

_Enter **3 to 5** pain points or customer phrases, **one per line** (bullets optional). Example: "Members can't check in when Wi‑Fi drops"_
"Members can't check in when Wi‑Fi drops

**Value & positioning (customer deck, FAQ, ICP)** · Question 14 of 42

What type of customer is NOT a fit?
customer deck

**Value & positioning (customer deck, FAQ, ICP)** · Question 15 of 42

Describe the ideal customer profile.

_Include business size, revenue range, operational maturity, member count, and typical use case._
business size 1200, revenue range 100k$, operational maturity, member count, and typical use case.

**Pricing & commercials (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 16 of 42

How is the partner priced?

_Include monthly fees, per-location pricing, usage tiers, referral-only pricing, discounts, bundles, or N/A._
monthly fees

**Pricing & commercials (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 17 of 42

Are there ABC-exclusive discounts or bundles?
yes

**Pricing & commercials (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 18 of 42

What commissions, referral fees, spiffs, or booking incentives apply?
none

**Pricing & commercials (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 19 of 42

Are there natural upsell or cross-sell opportunities?
cross sell

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 20 of 42

What is the official integration name?
payments processing partner

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 21 of 42

What is the target integration go-live date?

_Use an ISO date (2026-06-01) or a phrase like “early June”._
early June

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 22 of 42

Which ABC products are integrated?

Options (reply with one line, comma-separated): Ignite, GloFox, Trainerize, Evo, GymSales
Ignite

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 23 of 42

Describe how the integration works from the customer perspective.

_Explain what flows between systems, where setup happens, and what the customer sees day-to-day._
flows between systems, where setup happens, and what the customer sees day-to-day.

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 24 of 42

What setup model applies?

Options: Self-Serve · Partner-Assisted · ABC-Assisted
Self-Serve

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 25 of 42

What prerequisites are required before activation? (reply **None** if not applicable, otherwise use a short bullet list — one item per line)
none

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 26 of 42

What data is exchanged between ABC and the partner? (reply **None** if not applicable, otherwise use a short bullet list — one item per line)
none

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 27 of 42

What known limitations or constraints exist? (reply **None** if not applicable, otherwise use a short bullet list — one item per line)
limited

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 28 of 42

What common issues or errors occur and how are they resolved? (reply **None** if not applicable, otherwise use a short bullet list — one item per line)
no issues

**Integration & technical (Data Brief, Integration Guide)** · Question 29 of 42

Who handles integration support?
abc

**Activation & operations (Integration Guide, Process Guide)** · Question 30 of 42

Describe the onboarding and activation process end-to-end.
onboaridng is end to end

**Activation & operations (Integration Guide, Process Guide)** · Question 31 of 42

ABC owns
everything

**Activation & operations (Integration Guide, Process Guide)** · Question 32 of 42

Partner owns
process guide

**Activation & operations (Integration Guide, Process Guide)** · Question 33 of 42

Customer owns
talktrack

**Activation & operations (Integration Guide, Process Guide)** · Question 34 of 42

How long does onboarding typically take?
2 weeks

**Sales workflow (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 35 of 42

Which sales motion applies?

Options (reply with one line, comma-separated): Referral, Resell, Both
referral

**Sales workflow (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 36 of 42

How does the referral or sales process work?

_Include referral steps, routing, tracking, and post-submission customer experience._
end to end

**Sales workflow (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 37 of 42

What happens after a referral is submitted?
the work begins

**Sales workflow (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 38 of 42

How can reps track referral status?
thru website

**Sales workflow (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 39 of 42

What sales or support materials are available? (reply **None** if not applicable, otherwise use a short bullet list — one item per line)
none

**Support & contacts (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 40 of 42

How should support tickets be logged?

_Include product category, case type, required fields, and what gets escalated to the partner._
thru portal

**Support & contacts (FAQ, Process Guide)** · Question 41 of 42

Who are the primary contacts? (minimum 3)

_Enter **exactly 3** contacts, **one per line**, using this pattern: **Type | Name | Title | Email | Phone or Slack**. Example: Partner Contact | Jane Doe | VP Sales | jane@partner.com | @jane-partner_
Ignite | western | VP | Integration@abc | @ignite

**Social proof (customer deck)** · Question 42 of 42

Do you have a customer quote, testimonial, case study, or measurable outcome?

_Example: “We saved 10 hours/week on admin.” — Gym Owner, Denver. Enter TBD if unavailable._
We saved 10 hours/week on admin.” — Gym Owner, Denver. Enter TBD if unavailable.

communications.title: Coming Soon
communications.subject: Coming soon: Northbeam Advisors × ABC
communications.body:
## Coming soon: Northbeam Advisors × ABC

We're a few weeks out from launching our integration with **Northbeam Advisors** (Go-To-Market). Here's the short version so you can start positioning it.

**Why it matters**
Northbeam is a fast-growing GTM advisory shop that lands ABC inside mid-market deals via outbound referral. Their team brings warm intros and senior-level relationships across the revenue stack.

**Who it's for**
Mid-market companies (500–5,000 employees) actively re-platforming their revenue stack, where Northbeam is already advising on tooling decisions.

**Key dates**
- Beta with anchor customers: see launch timeline
- Launch readiness review: see launch timeline
- Public launch: 5 June 2026

The full Prepare-for-Launch packet (process guide, integration guide, FAQ, internal talk tracks) drops one week out. Reply here with questions.

communications.title: Prepare for Launch
communications.subject: Get ready: Northbeam Advisors × ABC launches 5 June 2026
communications.body:
## Prepare for launch: Northbeam Advisors × ABC

We launch in one week. Use this packet to get fully ready.

**Integration overview (from partner)**
flows between systems, where setup happens, and what the customer sees day-to-day.

**What's attached**
- Process Guide — how to position, qualify, and hand off
- Integration Guide — technical reference for solutions and support
- FAQ — top 10 questions with crisp answers
- Internal Talk Tracks — for AEs, CSMs, and support

**Action items**
- AEs: review the talk track and the ICP one-liner.
- CS: review the integration guide; flag accounts already asking for this.
- Support: scan the FAQ; ping us with anything unclear before launch day.

Questions? Reply here.

communications.title: New Partner is Live
communications.subject: Northbeam Advisors × ABC is live
communications.body:
## Northbeam Advisors × ABC is live

The integration is live as of today.

**The short pitch**
Northbeam is a fast-growing GTM advisory shop that lands ABC inside mid-market deals via outbound referral. Their team brings warm intros and senior-level relationships across the revenue stack.

**Resources (all in one place)**
- Customer-facing deck
- External one-pager
- All internal templates (process, integration, FAQ, talk tracks)

**Who to route to**
- Active opportunities: AEs can lead with the joint value prop on accounts that fit the ICP.
- Existing customers: CS team has a target list of accounts already showing intent signals.

We'll share a 30-day readout with metrics and customer stories. Until then — go sell it.

research.valueProp: Helix is a natural extension of ABC's platform, bundling identity + access intelligence on top of ABC. Sold together, joint customers consolidate two budget lines.
research.idealCustomerProfile: Upper-mid-market and enterprise security teams (2,000+ employees) already standardized on a modern IdP, with mature audit programs.
research.archetypeRationale: Helix is sold alongside ABC. Resell motion expands ACV/LTV. Monetization via margin / reseller economics.
research.scope: Phase 1: 3 lighthouse joint sells in 90 days. Phase 2: SKU bundling in ABC's reseller catalog.
research.competitiveLandscape: Adjacent vendors exist but Helix's depth in access intelligence is differentiated.

internalAwareness.subject: [Partnerships] We've signed Helix Platform — Platform
internalAwareness.body:
## Overview
Helix Platform is signed as a Platform partner — resell motion.

## Value Prop
Bundle ABC with Helix's access intelligence layer for consolidated security spend.

## Ideal Customer Profile
2,000+ employee security teams on a modern IdP with mature audit programs.

## Partner Archetype — Platform
Resell motion. Monetization: margin/reseller. KPIs: ARR expansion, ADS/attach rate, LTV.

## Scope
Phase 1: 3 lighthouse joint sells in 90 days. Phase 2: bundling in reseller catalog.

## What to expect next
Launch timeline + 3 launch communications in motion.

followUps.message: Gentle nudge: Sam from QuietChannel hasn't replied in 10 days. The Partner team has been notified.

Escalation: 30 days without a response from QuietChannel. Recommend pausing internal launch comms and re-confirming partner commitment.

research.valueProp: QuietChannel is a developer-tooling vendor whose users overlap heavily with ABC's ICP. Joint go-to-market drives inbound leads from QuietChannel into ABC.
research.idealCustomerProfile: Series B–D companies (100–800 employees) standardizing on modern developer infra.
research.archetypeRationale: QuietChannel reaches the same buyer and drives inbound leads. Monetized on gross revenue.
research.scope: Co-marketing + inbound lead routing in Phase 1.

internalAwareness.subject: [Partnerships] We've signed acme data — Data Affiliate
internalAwareness.body:
## Overview
We've signed **acme data** ([acmedata.com](https://acmedata.com)) as a new Data Affiliate partner. The integration moves into onboarding this week with primary contact **Jordan Lee** (jordan@acmedata.com).

## Value Prop
acme data accelerates data and analytics teams workflows with governed activation across the modern data stack. Integrated with ABC, joint customers get a unified path from warehouse model to operational system, reducing manual reconciliation and shrinking time-to-value by an estimated 30–40%.

## Ideal Customer Profile
Mid-market and upper-mid-market data and analytics teams (200–5,000 employees) running Snowflake, BigQuery, or Databricks with a downstream activation layer. Strong fit for teams with a dedicated analytics engineering or DataOps lead who already operate on a modern data stack and need fragmented activation surfaces and brittle pipelines resolved.

## Partner Archetype — Data Affiliate
acme data's value lands inside the product through a technical integration, driving stickiness and retention on mutual customers. Sales motion is integration-first, monetization typically via API fee.

## Scope
Phase 1 (60 days): production-grade API integration with ABC, joint co-marketing landing page, and one anchor customer reference. Phase 2 (post-launch): expanded acmedata↔ABC sync surface, in-product cross-promotion, and quarterly business reviews.

## What to expect next
- Partner-facing launch chat goes out today; we'll capture integration details, target launch date, and any partner-supplied collateral.
- The agent will follow up if the partner is unresponsive at 10 / 20 / 30 days and notify this team.
- Once the partner closes the chat, we'll publish a launch timeline with three communications (Coming Soon, Prepare for Launch, Live) plus customer-facing collateral.

Reply here with edits or call out blockers before we hit "Approve & Launch to Partner".
